# SBOM fixtures

Fixtures for `scripts/sbom-check.sh` and
`scripts/sbom-scripts-test.sh`. Kept committed so the self-test
runs without invoking `cargo-cyclonedx` — the test is pure bash

- jq, so contributors and CI can run it with no cargo on PATH.

## Files

| File                                | Purpose                                                                                                                                             |
| ----------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `valid.json`                        | A real SBOM from the pinned tool version against `mango-proto`. Regen via `scripts/sbom-gen-fixtures.sh`.                                           |
| `invalid-missing-spec-version.json` | `.specVersion` deleted — must fail assertion 3.                                                                                                     |
| `invalid-wrong-spec-version.json`   | `.specVersion == "1.3"` — must fail assertion 3 (tool default leak).                                                                                |
| `invalid-wrong-format.json`         | `.bomFormat == "SPDX"` — must fail assertion 2.                                                                                                     |
| `invalid-empty-components.json`     | `.components == []` — valid per-file shape; must fail the integration "non-empty floor" assertion when applied to `mango-proto` or `xtask-vet-ttl`. |

## Regenerating `valid.json`

Only regenerate when bumping `CARGO_CYCLONEDX_VERSION` in
[`.github/workflows/sbom.yml`](../../.github/workflows/sbom.yml).
The procedure is documented in [`docs/sbom-policy.md`](../../docs/sbom-policy.md#bump-procedure).

```bash
bash scripts/sbom-gen-fixtures.sh
```

The script reads the pin from the workflow, installs-check, and
regenerates `valid.json` from a real `cargo cyclonedx` run under
`SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1` (so the fixture is byte-stable).

The four `invalid-*.json` fixtures are hand-crafted mutations of
`valid.json`. They are NOT regenerated by the script because
each one is a targeted violation for a specific assertion — a
regenerated invalid fixture would accidentally fix itself.
